memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Backup
memory_items.lesson: Always copy the source workbook to a new output path before any modifications
memory_items.generalization: Preserve file integrity by creating a backup copy before manipulating cells, styles, or formulas

memory_items.title: Formula String Syntax
memory_items.lesson: Write Excel formulas as raw strings with '=' prefix, not evaluated values
memory_items.generalization: Store formula text literally so Excel recalculates on open; avoid pre-computing results

memory_items.title: Workbook Structure Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Programmatically check sheet names, dimensions, and cell content types before manipulation
memory_items.generalization: Validate workspace state before execution to identify correct sheets, ranges, and data characteristics

memory_items.title: Row Boundary Handling
memory_items.lesson: Distinguish header rows from data rows; apply different logic for first data cell vs subsequent cells
memory_items.generalization: Implement conditional logic based on position when operations differ for edge cases

memory_items.title: Post-Write Verification
memory_items.lesson: Reload the workbook after saving to confirm formulas and values persist correctly
memory_items.generalization: Verify output integrity by reading back saved content before reporting completion

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Formula Preservation**: Write formulas as raw strings with '=' prefix so Excel evaluates them dynamically.
- **First-Row Special Cases**: When implementing sequential or grouped logic, handle the first data row separately (start value vs recursive formula).
- **Double-Check Workflow**: Inspect → Modify → Verify by reloading ensures no silent failures in formula persistence.
- **Tool Synergy**: Use pandas for exploratory analysis + openpyxl for formula/style preservation in complex tasks.
- **Range Extraction**: Map metadata answer positions (like cell ranges) directly to loop bounds with clear boundary comments.
